data.body: I could be wrong, but the -w appears to stem from a past tense of the Middle English splaien, shortened form of displaien from Anglo-Norman despleien, Medieval Latin displicāre (pres. inf. displicō) from classical dis- and plicō, from proto-indo-European *plek. No mangling, just evolution!
data.author: did:plc:34foecukuzkuhexh7k4anqqk

data.body:
After 48-ish hours trying Wayland on my daily driver, I think I'm switching it back to X11.  Nothing broke, I just don't feel like re-setting up all the customizations I already had.

I'm still using Wayland on various other computers, though.

"A night you got robbed like a cat and went on a goose chase," she paused and met eyes with Silver "or as one night with me?"
